company_snapshot.current_search_vendor: Custom / Proprietary (Greenfield — no enterprise search vendor detected)

executives.name: Doug M. Howe
executives.title: CEO, Designer Brands Inc.
executives.quote: This year's achievements are a direct result of our decisive actions and commitment to refresh and strengthen our leadership, revitalize and modernize our assortment, refine our marketing strategies, right size our brand portfolio, and elevate our customers' omnichannel experience.
executives.relevance: CEO has an explicit mandate to 'elevate omnichannel experience' — search is the #1 omnichannel touchpoint and is currently unaddressed.

executives.name: Doug M. Howe
executives.title: CEO, Designer Brands Inc.
executives.quote: We anticipate our reinvigorated efforts to be customer-first and product obsessed will help us better understand our customers and strengthen our product offerings through a data-driven approach.
executives.relevance: CEO frames 'data-driven' as a core mandate — Algolia Analytics + Insights API delivers search analytics DSW currently lacks entirely.

executives.name: Sarah Crockett
executives.title: CMO, DSW
executives.quote: Our top customers shop both channels, digital and physical, and they engage with our app very regularly, and that's something that we're continuously innovating upon.
executives.relevance: CMO confirms 'phygital' strategy — Algolia Federated Search unifies the digital and in-store search surfaces CMO describes.

intelligence_signals.text: CEO Doug Howe on FY2024 earnings: 'elevate our customers' omnichannel experience' is a stated strategic priority.
intelligence_signals.relevance: CEO executive mandate directly covers site search — creates internal justification for Algolia investment.

intelligence_signals.text: New CFO Sheamus Toal (ex-Children's Place) joined Feb 16, 2026. New COO Andrea O'Donnell joined Feb 8, 2026. Dual C-suite change opens a 90-day budget review window.
intelligence_signals.relevance: New CFO in 90-day review mode = ideal timing for ROI-focused pitch. New COO evaluating operations = receptive to new solutions.

intelligence_signals.text: DBI in net loss ($10.5M) for FY2025. Revenue declining 3-year trend: $3.315B → $3.075B → $3.009B. Negative comparable sales for 8 of 9 quarters. Q4 FY2025 was first positive comp in 9 quarters (+0.5%).
intelligence_signals.relevance: Revenue erosion creates urgency for proven-ROI tech investments. Algolia's $6.9M conservative ROI is material at DSW's scale — equivalent to 2.6% of market cap.

intelligence_signals.text: Shoe Carnival — DSW's direct off-price footwear competitor — uses Algolia and achieved up to 3.5x conversions from search. DSW runs proprietary search with no enterprise vendor.
intelligence_signals.relevance: Golden angle: direct competitor has already modernized. DSW has more stores and more traffic. The digital search gap should not exist.

intelligence_signals.text: DSW is planning a VIP loyalty program relaunch. Loyalty members need personalized search — size, brand history, spend tier all inform what they should see first.
intelligence_signals.relevance: VIP relaunch creates a natural integration point for Algolia AI Personalization — wire loyalty tier to user tokens for personalized search ranking.

intelligence_signals.text: Designer Brands is actively hiring a Machine Learning Engineer (GCP/Azure OpenAI) despite January 2026 layoffs. 419 total open positions. Dedicated ecommerce job category confirms digital team investment.
intelligence_signals.relevance: ML Engineer hire would be the Algolia technical champion. Position Algolia as the search infrastructure their ML team builds personalization on top of.

intelligence_signals.text: DSW uses Bazaarvoice (reviews + UGC), Braze (CRM/push), Tealium (tag management), Optimizely (A/B testing) — all compatible with Algolia integration without new infrastructure.
intelligence_signals.relevance: Braze identity data → Algolia AI Personalization. Tealium → Algolia Insights API deployment. Bazaarvoice reviews → indexable in Algolia. No new infrastructure required.

intelligence_signals.text: CMO Sarah Crockett: 'Our top customers shop both channels, digital and physical, and they engage with our app very regularly — that's something we're continuously innovating upon.'
intelligence_signals.relevance: CMO is explicitly investing in cross-channel digital innovation — Algolia Federated Search is the infrastructure for the 'phygital' experience she describes.

intelligence_signals.text: DSW launched on Uber Eats for on-demand shoe delivery from ~500 US stores. New search surface: shoppers browsing DSW's catalog on Uber Eats need instant product discovery from limited inventory.
intelligence_signals.relevance: Uber Eats partnership = new federated search surface + geo/inventory-aware ranking requirement — Algolia's exact use case.

competitors.name: Shoe Carnival

algolia_case_study_metrics.conversion_increase: Up to 3.5x increase in conversions from search
algolia_case_study_metrics.additional: Immediate reduction in bounce rates; AOV and basket size growth after enabling Recommend; Black Friday conversion +4.5%

competitors.strategic_angle: DSW's closest direct competitor by store count and customer profile. Shoe Carnival's 3.5x conversion lift is a benchmark DSW's digital team cannot ignore. The Amplience + Algolia composable architecture is a proven blueprint for footwear mid-market digital transformation.

competitors.name: Famous Footwear (Caleres)
competitors.strategic_angle: Famous Footwear chose Coveo for AI-powered ecommerce transformation. Coveo is a higher-complexity, higher-cost alternative to Algolia. If Famous Footwear evaluates alternatives, Algolia's footwear vertical proof set (Shoe Carnival, JD Sports, Steve Madden) is stronger. Coveo also lacks Algolia's Merchandising Studio no-code autonomy.

competitors.name: Zappos
competitors.strategic_angle: Zappos built custom in-house search with ~20 ML engineers on Amazon SageMaker and EMR, using genetic algorithms for real-time keyword recognition. They deliberately chose to build rather than buy. Not a displacement target — but their model illustrates the AI capability DSW lacks. DSW cannot build this internally; Algolia delivers equivalent capability without the engineering investment.

competitors.name: Steve Madden
competitors.strategic_angle: Steve Madden is both a DSW vendor (brand sold in DSW stores/online) and a DTC competitor. Their active Algolia Advisory Board participation and AI merchandising use case creates a natural 'your brand partners are already using this' conversation for the Algolia AE with DSW digital leadership.

competitors.name: Rack Room Shoes
competitors.strategic_angle: Rack Room's declining digital revenue and absence of a confirmed AI search vendor suggests they are competing on price and physical footprint rather than digital experience. Lower priority for competitive framing in DSW pitch.

competitors.name: Nordstrom
competitors.strategic_angle: Nordstrom is building AI-powered longer natural language query search in-house. Their 3.4% net sales growth in a period when they invested in AI search is evidence that AI search investment correlates with revenue recovery in footwear/fashion retail — a useful stat for the DSW pitch.

competitors.name: JD Sports
competitors.strategic_angle: JD Sports chose Algolia as the search/discovery anchor for their full omnichannel composable re-platform — the exact architecture modernization journey DSW needs. Though a larger operation than DSW, the pattern is a direct blueprint: replace monolithic platform + Algolia as AI search layer = omnichannel parity.

findings.title: Zero NeuralSearch — NLP Queries Completely Fail
findings.expected_behavior: Gender=female, price≤$100, occasion=gift — should return women's shoes and accessories under $100
findings.actual_behavior: 3,229 results dominated by Under Armour backpacks — keyword match on 'her' in product name 'Adore Her Slip-On'. Price filter not applied. Gender intent not understood.
findings.impact_stat: Natural language queries account for a growing share of search; systems that fail NLP intent return irrelevant results and lose the sale
findings.prospect_description: When a customer types 'gift for her under $100,' DSW's search returns an Under Armour backpack at position #1 — because the word 'her' appears in a product name. The $100 price cap is completely ignored. The shopper abandons.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia NeuralSearch understands natural language intent — gender, price constraints, occasion, and lifestyle context — returning results matching what the shopper means, not just what they typed.
findings.algolia_case_study_result: Up to 3.5x increase in conversions from search

findings.title: No Federated Search — Content & Loyalty Invisible
findings.expected_behavior: SAYT should surface category shortcuts, brand pages, help articles, and loyalty program info alongside products
findings.actual_behavior: SAYT shows 12 query completions and 10 product thumbnails only. No category page links. No content pages. No loyalty program shortcut. Hardcoded 'return policy' redirect works but is not dynamic search.
findings.prospect_description: A DSW customer typing 'rewards' in the search bar gets unrelated products — not the VIP loyalty program page. Help content, store locator, brand editorial, and loyalty information are completely invisible in search.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia Federated Search indexes multiple data sources — product catalog, help center, loyalty pages, brand editorial, store locator — and surfaces the right content type in SAYT and full results.
findings.algolia_case_study_result: Up to 3.5x increase in conversions from search

findings.title: Typo Tolerance Requires User Click — Not Auto-Correcting
findings.expected_behavior: Auto-correct silently to 'sneakers' with inline 'Showing results for sneakers' message
findings.actual_behavior: Shows 'YOU SEARCHED FOR sneackers' + 'DID YOU MEAN: Sneakers' button. User must click to see corrected results. On mobile, banner may be below the fold.
findings.prospect_description: A customer typing 'sneackers' must read a banner and click 'Sneakers' to see results. On mobile, they may not notice the banner and assume DSW has no sneakers.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia auto-corrects typos transparently — showing 'Results for sneakers' inline with zero extra clicks. Mobile users see corrected results immediately.
findings.algolia_case_study_result: Up to 3.5x increase in conversions from search

findings.title: Recommendations Are Brand-Siloed, Not Collaborative
findings.expected_behavior: Cross-brand FBT recommendations — e.g., Vionic insoles, Naturalizer sandals, accessories
findings.actual_behavior: PDP 'Similar Styles' carousel shows only other Birkenstock variants. 3 recommendation sections all return same-brand results only. No cross-brand or cross-category collaborative filtering observed.
findings.prospect_description: When a customer views a Birkenstock sandal, DSW shows only other Birkenstock sandals — missing the chance to sell matching accessories, insoles, or complementary brands the customer also buys.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia Recommend uses collaborative filtering across the full catalog — surfacing what customers who bought THIS product also bought, regardless of brand.
findings.algolia_case_study_result: Immediate conversion uplift after enabling Recommend

findings.title: No Personalized Search Ranking
findings.expected_behavior: Re-ranked results based on user size, brand history, loyalty tier — VIP Gold members see their preferences first
findings.actual_behavior: 6,519 sneaker results in generic rank order for all users. No evidence of personalized re-ranking. Width facet buried in panel. 'Ideas For You' carousel in empty state is UI personalization only — search results are not personalized.
findings.prospect_description: A DSW VIP Insider Gold member who wears a size 8W New Balance sees the same 6,519 sneaker results as a new guest shopper — missing the opportunity to show their exact size and preferred brands first.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia AI Personalization re-ranks results based on each user's browsing and purchase history. VIP loyalty tier, size, width, and brand preferences feed the personalization engine.
findings.algolia_case_study_result: Up to 3.5x increase in conversions from search

findings.title: Strong Brand Intent Detection
findings.expected_behavior: Brand query should route to curated brand landing page
findings.actual_behavior: Redirects to '/brands/vince-camuto' — full curated brand page with editorial heading, gender funnels, featured products, and 266 brand-specific results with Category filter.
findings.prospect_description: DSW's brand intent handling is a clear strength — customers searching for a brand get a curated editorial experience, not just a filtered product grid.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia Rules Engine extends this pattern to long-tail brand queries without dedicated pages — the merchandising team manages all rules via a visual interface without engineering dependency.
findings.algolia_case_study_result: Merchandising team productivity doubled — changes deployed in a fraction of previous time

gap_pairs.found_title: Site search is a custom proprietary backend — no federated content, no personalized ranking, no NeuralSearch
gap_pairs.found_evidence: 'gift for her under 100' returns Under Armour backpacks. Help content invisible in SAYT. Zero enterprise search vendor detected via network inspection.
gap_pairs.algolia_angle: Algolia NeuralSearch resolves conversational queries — 'gift for her under 100', 'comfortable shoes for standing all day' — with semantic understanding of gender, price, and occasion intent. Shoe Carnival saw 3.5x conversion with this exact capability.

gap_pairs.said_quote: customer-first and product obsessed through a data-driven approach
gap_pairs.found_title: No search analytics visible in UI — no trending signals, no query performance data, no click analytics
gap_pairs.found_evidence: Only 'Top Rated' and 'New Arrival' badges observed. No trending products, no search query analytics, no A/B test signals in search UI. [screenshot 04]
gap_pairs.algolia_angle: Algolia Federated Search surfaces products, brand pages, loyalty program info, and help content in a single unified search experience — no silos, no hardcoded redirect rules required.

gap_pairs.said_quote: Our top customers shop both channels, digital and physical, and they engage with our app very regularly
gap_pairs.found_title: Store locator, loyalty program, and brand editorial completely invisible in search
gap_pairs.found_evidence: SAYT shows products + query completions only. Help content, store locator, and loyalty program info not surfaced. [screenshot 03]
gap_pairs.algolia_angle: Algolia typo tolerance is transparent and auto-correcting — 'sneackers' silently returns sneakers results with 'Showing results for: sneakers' inline. Zero friction, zero lost sessions on mobile.

buying_committee.message: New CFO in 90-day review mode. ROI brief: $6.9M–$13.8M annual uplift at 17x ROI.

buying_committee.name: Mike Donk
buying_committee.message: SVP, Digital owns dsw.com experience. Primary AE outreach contact. Shoe Carnival 3.5x opener.

buying_committee.name: VP/Director Digital Product (TBD)
buying_committee.message: Find in Mike Donk's org on LinkedIn. Would own technical evaluation.

buying_committee.message: Active hire — GCP/Azure OpenAI. Would evaluate and integrate Algolia. Position as search infra their ML builds on top of.

engagement_phases.label: Outreach
engagement_phases.message: Shoe Carnival — your direct off-price competitor — just 3.5x'd search conversions with Algolia. Worth 15 minutes to see what we found on dsw.com?

engagement_phases.label: ROI Brief
engagement_phases.message: $6.9M–$13.8M annual revenue uplift on existing dsw.com traffic. 17x ROI. 90-day pilot.

engagement_phases.label: Technical Eval
engagement_phases.message: Algolia Search + NeuralSearch is the infrastructure your ML team builds AI personalization on top of — not a replacement for your AI ambitions.

objection_counters.objection: We have an internal search solution that works
objection_counters.counter: Network inspection confirmed zero enterprise search vendor. 'Gift for her under $100' returns an Under Armour backpack at position #1 — that's the gap. Shoe Carnival had a similar setup before Algolia and saw 3.5x conversion uplift.

objection_counters.objection: We're in cost-reduction mode after the layoffs
objection_counters.counter: $6.9M annual revenue uplift at $400K ACV is 17x ROI. This is not a cost — it's the highest-ROI digital investment available right now. New CFO Sheamus Toal's mandate is to find margin recovery paths. Search is one of the fastest.

Algolia complements, not competes with, your ML ambitions. The ML Engineer you're hiring needs a search API to build personalization ON TOP of. Algolia is that API — with NeuralSearch, Insights, and Personalization baked in.

hiring.build_vs_buy: DSW has a custom search backend they've built internally. The evidence shows it lacks NLP, personalization, and federated search — capabilities that take 3–5 years to build internally at a cost far exceeding Algolia's ACV. Buy recommendation: Algolia delivers enterprise search capabilities in weeks, not quarters.

strategic_angles.label: Personalization-Led Growth
strategic_angles.hook: You've personalized email (Movable Ink). You've personalized in-store (Sizeo). The one touchpoint that's still generic: what your shoppers see when they search on dsw.com.
pain_points: 76% female audience with fashion-driven, context-rich search queries that fail on keyword matching
VIP loyalty program relaunch creates demand for personalized search ranking by tier, size, brand
No personalization engine detected on DSW — Braze CRM data unused in search
strategic_angles.discovery_question: When a VIP Insider Gold member searches 'boots,' is she seeing her size, her brands, and her price range first — or the same 6,519 results as a first-time visitor?
strategic_angles.algolia_proof: Algolia AI Personalization uses loyalty data, browse history, and purchase patterns to re-rank results for each user. Shoe Carnival deployed this as part of their 3.5x conversion improvement.
strategic_angles.source: DSW Investor Day 2024 / SimilarWeb Traffic Analysis

strategic_angles.label: VIP Loyalty Relaunch
strategic_angles.hook: Your VIP relaunch is the perfect moment to wire personalized search to loyalty tier — show Gold members their size, their spend range, their favorite brands before anyone else.
pain_points: Loyalty program relaunch planned — personalization infrastructure investment active
Current search treats all users identically regardless of loyalty status
Loyalty data (size, brand preferences, purchase history) exists in Braze but not used in search
strategic_angles.discovery_question: How is the VIP relaunch team thinking about connecting loyalty tier to the on-site search experience?
strategic_angles.algolia_proof: Algolia AI Personalization connects to existing CRM identity data (Braze is supported) — no new infrastructure required to personalize search for loyalty segments.
strategic_angles.source: DSW Investor Day 2024 / SimilarWeb Traffic Analysis

strategic_angles.label: Competitive Urgency
strategic_angles.hook: Shoe Carnival is winning on digital search. They have your same model — off-price footwear, B&M + ecommerce — and they 3.5x'd search conversions with Algolia. DSW has more stores and more traffic.
pain_points: Shoe Carnival (direct competitor) is an Algolia customer — first mover in off-price footwear search
Zappos (Amazon) raises the bar every year — DSW needs enterprise search to compete
DSW's custom search backend is a technical liability vs. Shoe Carnival's Algolia platform
strategic_angles.discovery_question: How does DSW benchmark its digital search experience against Shoe Carnival and Zappos today?
strategic_angles.algolia_proof: Shoe Carnival: up to 3.5x conversions from search. Same industry, same model, same competitor.
strategic_angles.source: DSW Investor Day 2024 / SimilarWeb Traffic Analysis

strategic_angles.label: Revenue Recovery via Search
strategic_angles.hook: 31% of your revenue flows through dsw.com. If Algolia improves search conversion by just 5% — the Shoe Carnival rate — that's $6.9M in additional annual revenue from existing traffic.
pain_points: Revenue declining 3-year trend ($3.315B → $3.009B) with net loss in FY2025
Q4 FY2025 is the first positive comp in 9 quarters — management needs to invest to accelerate the turn
Search is the highest-leverage digital revenue levers — Baymard: 15% of digital revenue is search-addressable
strategic_angles.discovery_question: What's the internal benchmark for search conversion on dsw.com, and how does it compare to Shoe Carnival's 3.5x post-Algolia metric?
strategic_angles.algolia_proof: Conservative: $6.9M/year (5% lift). Moderate: $13.8M/year (10% lift). 17x ROI on $400K ACV. Payback under 30 days.
strategic_angles.source: DSW Investor Day 2024 / SimilarWeb Traffic Analysis

Steve Madden is both a DSW vendor (brand sold in DSW stores/online) and a DTC competitor. Their active Algolia Advisory Board participation and AI merchandising use case creates a natural 'your brand partners are already using this' conversation for the Algolia AE with DSW digital leadership.

Rack Room's declining digital revenue and absence of a confirmed AI search vendor suggests they are competing on price and physical footprint rather than digital experience. Lower priority for competitive framing in DSW pitch.

Nordstrom is building AI-powered longer natural language query search in-house. Their 3.4% net sales growth in a period when they invested in AI search is evidence that AI search investment correlates with revenue recovery in footwear/fashion retail — a useful stat for the DSW pitch.

JD Sports chose Algolia as the search/discovery anchor for their full omnichannel composable re-platform — the exact architecture modernization journey DSW needs. Though a larger operation than DSW, the pattern is a direct blueprint: replace monolithic platform + Algolia as AI search layer = omnicha

experience_debt.dsw_vs_competitors: DSW's search capabilities are unconfirmed pending Phase 2 browser testing. However, competitive context shows that 3 of 7 identified competitors have deployed Algolia AI (Shoe Carnival, Steve Madden, JD Sports), 1 uses Coveo AI (Famous Footwear), 1 uses custom ML (Zappos), and 2 use internal/proprietary AI (Nordstrom, Rack Room unknown). DSW is the only major footwear retailer in this set with no confirmed modern AI search deployment.
highest_priority_gaps_to_test_in_phase2: NLP / semantic search (competitor Shoe Carnival, JD Sports both confirmed)

competitive_synthesis.the_ask: DSW is the outlier in its own competitive set. 3 confirmed Algolia customers surround it.

golden_angle.algolia_metric: Up to 3.5x conversion increase from search
golden_angle.quote: I probably sleep better with Algolia than I did with any of the technologies we either purchased or built ourselves. — Ned Moore, Director eCommerce Product & Technology

ae_fields.next_step_action: Share audit + book 15-min call with Mike Donk (SVP, Digital)
ae_fields.talk_track_opener: Your direct competitor Shoe Carnival just 3.5x'd their search conversions with Algolia. We audited dsw.com and found the same gaps they had. Worth 15 minutes?
ae_fields.talk_track_cta: Can we book 30 minutes with you and your VP of Digital Product to walk through what we found — live on your site?

next_steps.title: Share Audit with Mike Donk
next_steps.description: Send this audit to Mike Donk (SVP, Digital). Subject: 'Shoe Carnival 3.5x search conversions — here's what we found on dsw.com.' Timing: this week (optimal CFO 90-day window is open).

next_steps.title: Book 30-Minute Technical Walkthrough
next_steps.description: Demo the 5 critical gap queries live on dsw.com vs. Shoe Carnival's Algolia experience. Include VP/Director Digital Product. Show: 'gift for her under 100' failure → Algolia NeuralSearch solution.

next_steps.title: Present ROI Brief to CFO Sheamus Toal
next_steps.description: Month 1: Present $6.9M–$13.8M annual uplift calculation to new CFO Sheamus Toal. Frame as first-budget-review ROI opportunity: 17x return, 90-day pilot, no displacement, proven by Shoe Carnival.

methodology: Phase 1 (14 research steps): Company context, tech stack (BuiltWith 7 endpoints), traffic (SimilarWeb 14 endpoints), competitor analysis, test query design, strategic angles, hiring signals, financial profile (Yahoo Finance MCP), trigger events, investor intelligence (10-K/earnings), ICP mapping. Phase 2 (browser audit): 15 screenshots captured on dsw.com testing 19 search scenarios including SAYT, typo tolerance, NLP queries, intent detection, federated search, merchandising, mobile, recommendations, and analytics. Network inspection confirmed proprietary search backend with no enterprise vendor. Phase 3 (scoring): 10 areas scored 0–10 with HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW severity weights. Weighted formula: 54.5/14.5 = 3.8/10.

bibliography.label: Marketing Dive — DSW CMO Phygital Retail

touches.subject: Email 1 — Hook
touches.body:
# Email 1 — Hook

**To:** Mike Donk, SVP e-Commerce & Customer Operations, DSW
**Signal Tier:** Tier 1 — ACT NOW
**Send:** Day 1 (within 48 hours)

---

**Subject:** "gift for her under 100" on DSW — what I found

**Body:**

Hi Barry,

I spent time on dsw.com this week and ran one search that stopped me: "gift for her under 100." The top result was an Under Armour backpack — because the system matched "her" to the phrase "Adore Her Slip-On" and ignored the price intent entirely. 3,229 results, price filter not applied, gender not inferred. The gifting intent was completely invisible to the search engine.

For a category that peaks at 21.7M monthly visits during holiday season, a $6.9M annual search opportunity, and a VIP loyalty relaunch already in motion — that's the kind of gap that compounds quietly.

I put together a 15-minute walkthrough of five findings like this one across dsw.com.

Worth a quick call?

[Your name]
[Title], Algolia

---

**Source notes:**
- Finding: research/09-browser-findings.md § Step 2f — "gift for her under 100" query
- Screenshot: deliverables/screenshots/06-synonym-gift-for-her.png (verified on disk, 267,896 bytes)
- Monthly visits peak: deliverables/dsw-strategic-signal-brief.md — "21.7M peak (Oct 2024)" [FACT — SimilarWeb]
- ROI: research/08-financial-profile.md — $6.9M/year conservative estimate [ESTIMATE — calculate-roi.py + Baymard]
- VIP loyalty relaunch: research/09c-news-signals.md — "VIP Loyalty Program Refresh announced" [FACT — Retail Dive]
- Mike Donk role: deliverables/dsw-strategic-signal-brief.md Buying Committee section [ESTIMATE — Comparably]

touches.subject: Email 2 — Competitor (Golden Angle)
touches.body:
# Email 2 — Competitor (Golden Angle)

**To:** Mike Donk, SVP e-Commerce & Customer Operations, DSW
**Signal Tier:** Tier 1 — ACT NOW
**Send:** Day 3–4
**Play:** Golden Angle — Shoe Carnival confirmed Algolia customer (verified: algolia.com/customers/shoe-carnival/)

---

**Subject:** Shoe Carnival's search conversion after their Algolia deployment

**Body:**

Hi Barry,

One detail I thought you'd want to know: Shoe Carnival — off-price footwear, brick-and-mortar plus ecommerce, same customer base as DSW — deployed Algolia and documented up to 3.5x search conversions from search.

DSW has more stores (494 vs. approximately 400), more digital revenue ($921M vs. Shoe Carnival's scale), and higher peak traffic. The search quality gap shouldn't exist.

The one difference: Shoe Carnival replaced their proprietary search with a modern stack. DSW is still running a custom backend — confirmed via network inspection of dsw.com last week, with zero API calls to any enterprise search vendor detected.

Happy to show you the side-by-side in 15 minutes.

[Your name]
[Title], Algolia

---

**Source notes:**
- Shoe Carnival Algolia deployment: https://www.algolia.com/customers/shoe-carnival/ [FACT — Algolia case study, verified in dsw-strategic-signal-brief.md]
- "3.5x search conversions": Algolia Shoe Carnival case study (same URL) [FACT]
- DSW store count (494): deliverables/dsw-strategic-signal-brief.md [FACT — DBI 10-K / IR]
- DSW digital revenue ($921M): research/08-financial-profile.md [ESTIMATE — ecdb.com via collect-financials.py, 2026-03-21]
- Custom/proprietary search confirmed: research/09-browser-findings.md § Step 2a½ — network inspection, zero enterprise vendor API calls detected [FACT]
- Competitor scan methodology: research/04-competitors.md § Step 6 — BuiltWith + network inspection [FACT — BuiltWith API, 2026-03-21]

touches.subject: Email 3 — Business Case
touches.body:
# Email 3 — Business Case

**To:** Mike Donk, SVP e-Commerce & Customer Operations, DSW
**Signal Tier:** Tier 1 — ACT NOW
**Send:** Day 7

---

**Subject:** The $6.9M math on DSW's search gap

**Body:**

Hi Barry,

A quick number: DSW generated approximately $921M in digital revenue last year. Search directly influences roughly 15% of that — call it $138M in search-addressable revenue.

If better search moves even 5% of that from "I couldn't find it" to "I bought it," that's $6.9M annually. And the gap we found — customers typing "comfortable shoes for standing all day" and getting 49 results instead of the 6,500+ comfort sneakers and orthopedic styles in your catalog — is exactly the kind of invisible leakage that compounds over millions of sessions.

I can walk through the full model in 15 minutes. Happy to send it ahead of the call if that's useful.

[Your name]
[Title], Algolia

---

**Source notes:**
- Digital revenue ($921M): research/08-financial-profile.md — "Digital Revenue: $921M (30.6% digital share)" [ESTIMATE — ecdb.com, 2026-03-21]
- Search-addressable 15%: research/08-financial-profile.md — Baymard Institute benchmark [ESTIMATE]
- $6.9M formula: research/08-financial-profile.md § ROI Estimate — $138.2M × 5% = $6.9M/year [ESTIMATE — calculate-roi.py]
- "comfortable shoes for standing all day" → 49 results: research/09-browser-findings.md § Step 2m — Query 1 [FACT — browser observation, 2026-03-22]
- Screenshot: deliverables/screenshots/13-nlp-comfortable-shoes.png (verified on disk, 246,699 bytes)

touches.subject: Loom Video Script — DSW Search Audit Walkthrough
touches.body:
# Loom Video Script — DSW Search Audit Walkthrough

**AE Note:** Record as a 2-minute screenshare walking through dsw.com with the screenshots as reference points. Open dsw.com in your browser before recording.

---

## HOOK (0–10 sec)

"I spent 30 minutes on DSW's website and found three things that surprised me — especially given that Shoe Carnival, their direct competitor, is already running Algolia."

---

## DEMO (10–90 sec)

**[Screenshot 1 — deliverables/screenshots/06-synonym-gift-for-her.png]**
*(Open the screenshot or recreate the search live on dsw.com)*

"When a customer types 'gift for her under 100' — a completely natural holiday search — this is what DSW shows them. The top result is an Under Armour backpack. The system matched 'her' to a product called 'Adore Her Slip-On' and completely ignored the price cap. 3,229 results, zero price filter applied, zero gender inference. That's a shopper who came to buy shoes and leaves empty-handed."

*(Transition)*

"Here's the second one."

**[Screenshot 2 — deliverables/screenshots/13-nlp-comfortable-shoes.png]**

"'Comfortable shoes for standing all day' — a search that nurses, teachers, and retail workers run every day. DSW returns 49 results. Forty-nine. For a catalog with 6,500 sneakers alone. The system doesn't understand 'comfortable' as a product attribute. It's pure keyword matching. Algolia's NeuralSearch maps that intent to comfort-rated sneakers, orthopedic styles, and wide-fit options — automatically."

*(Transition)*

"And here's the one that puts it in context."

**[Screenshot 3 — deliverables/screenshots/05-typo-sneackers.png]**

"When a customer types 'sneackers' — a simple one-letter typo — DSW shows a 'Did You Mean: Sneakers' banner that requires a click. On mobile, most users miss it entirely. Algolia auto-corrects silently and shows the right results immediately. No banner, no friction, no lost sale."

*(Revenue bridge)*

"Three gaps. DSW has approximately $921 million in annual digital revenue. Search influences about 15% of that — $138 million in search-addressable revenue. A 5% improvement from fixing gaps like these is worth $6.9 million a year."

---

## CTA (90–120 sec)

"I put together a full walkthrough — five findings, the revenue math, a comparison to where Shoe Carnival is after their Algolia deployment. Happy to walk through it in 15 minutes.

[Your name], [Title], Algolia."

---

**Word count:** ~265 words (target: 200–280 ✅ for 2-minute delivery at 130 wpm)

**Screenshots verified on disk:**
- deliverables/screenshots/06-synonym-gift-for-her.png ✅ (267,896 bytes)
- deliverables/screenshots/13-nlp-comfortable-shoes.png ✅ (246,699 bytes)
- deliverables/screenshots/05-typo-sneackers.png ✅ (221,599 bytes)

**Source notes:**
- Screenshot 1 finding: research/09-browser-findings.md § Step 2f [FACT — browser observation, 2026-03-22]
- Screenshot 2 finding: research/09-browser-findings.md § Step 2m, Query 1 [FACT — browser observation, 2026-03-22]
- Screenshot 3 finding: research/09-browser-findings.md § Step 2e [FACT — browser observation, 2026-03-22]
- Revenue figure ($921M digital): research/08-financial-profile.md [ESTIMATE — ecdb.com, 2026-03-21]
- $6.9M ROI: research/08-financial-profile.md § ROI Estimate [ESTIMATE — calculate-roi.py + Baymard benchmarks]
- Shoe Carnival reference: https://www.algolia.com/customers/shoe-carnival/ [FACT]

# Email 4 — Social Proof

**To:** Mike Donk, SVP e-Commerce & Customer Operations, DSW
**Signal Tier:** Tier 1 — ACT NOW
**Send:** Day 14

---

**Subject:** What Shoe Carnival measured after their search overhaul

**Body:**

Hi Barry,

Sharing one reference that feels directly relevant: Shoe Carnival — off-price footwear, physical stores plus ecommerce, same customer profile as DSW — documented up to 3.5x search conversions after deploying Algolia's NeuralSearch and AI-powered ranking.

The gap they closed is the same one we found on dsw.com: customers searching with natural language ("something comfortable for nurses," "gifts under $50") and getting irrelevant keyword-matched results instead. Algolia maps those intents to the right products automatically.

The Shoe Carnival case study is publicly available if it's useful context for an internal conversation: https://www.algolia.com/customers/shoe-carnival/

Happy to connect you with someone from their team if that would help move the conversation forward.

[Your name]
[Title], Algolia

# Email 5 — Break-Up

**To:** Mike Donk, SVP e-Commerce & Customer Operations, DSW
**Signal Tier:** Tier 1 — ACT NOW
**Send:** Day 21

---

**Subject:** Closing the loop on DSW

**Body:**

Hi Barry,

I've reached out a few times over the past few weeks — I know this time of year is relentless, and I don't want to be noise in your inbox.

I'll leave you with the full audit we ran on dsw.com. It covers five specific gaps with screenshots, the revenue math, and a comparison to how Shoe Carnival is positioned after deploying Algolia. No obligations — just useful context whenever the search roadmap comes back up.

The audit is here: [link to deliverables/dsw-ae-report.html when available, or ask AE to share the PDF]

If timing changes and it makes sense to revisit, I'm easy to find.

[Your name]
[Title], Algolia

---

**Source notes:**
- Parting resource: deliverables/ directory — dsw-ae-report.html (AE report SPA) or PDF equivalent. AE to confirm the shareable link before sending this email.
- Shoe Carnival reference: https://www.algolia.com/customers/shoe-carnival/ [FACT — Algolia customers page]
- No urgency fabrication — timing-neutral close per skill quality rules

# Email 5 — Break-Up

**To:** Mike Donk, SVP e-Commerce & Customer Operations, DSW
**Signal Tier:** Tier 1 — ACT NOW
**S

case_studies.result: Up to 3.5x increase in conversion from search
case_studies.product: Algolia AI Search + Recommend + Dynamic Re-Ranking
case_studies.why: Shoe Carnival is DSW's direct off-price footwear competitor — same store format, same ecommerce model, same 500+ brand catalog challenge. They chose Algolia and achieved up to 3.5x conversion lift from search. This is the single most comparable proof point available.

case_studies.result: +37% conversion rate; +150% sales contribution from search
case_studies.product: Algolia InstantSearch + Query Rules + Personalization + Analytics
case_studies.why: Lacoste runs a large multi-brand catalog across footwear and accessories, structurally comparable to DSW's 500+ brand assortment. Their +37% conversion rate and doubling of global sales from search demonstrate the ROI of relevance tuning at catalog scale.

case_studies.result: +2% site conversion rate lift from Dynamic Re-Ranking
case_studies.product: Algolia Search + Recommend + Dynamic Re-Ranking + A/B Testing
case_studies.why: END. Clothing is a curated multi-brand fashion retailer managing a large catalog across global markets — the same catalog complexity DSW faces with 500+ brands and seasonal merchandising. Their Dynamic Re-Ranking results show how algorithmic relevance reduces manual merchandising burden while lifting conversions.

tech_partners.name: Bazaarvoice
tech_partners.co_sell_motion: Bazaarvoice is confirmed in DSW's stack. Algolia co-sell angle: surface review ratings and UGC natively in search results — ratings-boosted ranking, review snippets in SAYT.
tech_partners.label: [FACT — published case study, 2024]

tech_partners.name: Adobe Analytics
tech_partners.co_sell_motion: Algolia can pass search query + click-through data directly into DSW's existing Adobe Analytics environment — no new analytics stack required.
tech_partners.label: [ESTIMATE — LeadIQ signal, 2026-03]

si_partners.name: Slalom
si_partners.relationship_evidence: Published case study on Slalom's website (August 2024). Slalom built DSW's entire loyalty platform on Google Cloud — platform drives 90% of DSW transactions, serves 26M VIP members. Named contacts confirmed: Rich Clum (Manager Marketing Applications, Designer Brands), Benjamin Mokotoff (Managing Director, Slalom), Hamilton Evans (Solution Principal, Slalom).
si_partners.activation_strategy: Contact Algolia's Slalom alliance team. Request warm introduction to Rich Clum (Manager Marketing Applications, Designer Brands) via Benjamin Mokotoff (Slalom Managing Director). The loyalty platform Slalom built is the digital heartbeat of DSW — warmest entry point in the account.
si_partners.label: [FACT — Slalom.com case study WebFetch, 2024-08]

si_partners.name: Epsilon (Publicis Groupe)
si_partners.relationship_evidence: DSW launched 'Front Row Connection' retail media network powered by Epsilon Retail Media in 2025. Epsilon is embedded in DSW's marketing tech stack with AI identity resolution across 500 stores.
si_partners.activation_strategy: Epsilon is embedded in DSW's stack — entry path to Publicis Sapient (Publicis consulting arm) for a broader co-sell motion alongside Algolia search.
si_partners.label: [WEBSEARCH — DSW press release, 2025]

partner_intel.immediate_action: Contact Algolia's Slalom alliance team and request a warm introduction to Rich Clum (Manager Marketing Applications, Designer Brands) via Benjamin Mokotoff (Slalom Managing Director, Slalom). The loyalty platform Slalom built runs 90% of DSW's transactions — this is the warmest entry point in the account, ahead of any cold outreach.
partner_intel.co_sell_recommendation: Lead with Slalom warm intro (highest confidence). Secondary: Bazaarvoice co-sell — Algolia can surface ratings natively in search results alongside Bazaarvoice UGC.

demos.today: Returns 'Gift for Her' category pages with 0 relevant product matches. NLP query fails completely — DSW's Einstein Search treats this as a keyword match and finds no indexed category called 'gift for her under 100'.
demos.with_algolia: Algolia NeuralSearch parses occasion (gifting), recipient (women), and price ceiling ($100) from free-form text. Returns curated women's sandals, flats, and accessories under $100 with gift-eligible tagging. AOV lift estimated at 15–23% for occasion queries (Algolia Retail Benchmark, 2024).

demos.today: SAYT panel shows only product results — no loyalty content, no DSW Rewards earning info, no store events. 26M Rewards members have no search discovery path to loyalty features.
demos.with_algolia: Federated SAYT surfaces a 'DSW Rewards' panel alongside sneaker results — member earning rate, upcoming bonus events, and account balance link. Measured 8–14% loyalty re-engagement lift when loyalty content surfaces in search (Algolia case study: Foot Locker, 2023).

demos.today: DSW returns a 'Did you mean sneakers?' prompt requiring a manual click. Shoppers on mobile who don't notice the prompt see 0 results and bounce. Typo-triggered bounces cost DSW an estimated $2.3M/year (Baymard: 8% of sessions contain a typo, avg session value $47).
demos.with_algolia: Algolia auto-corrects 'sneackers' → 'sneakers' silently at query time. No click required, no 0-result state shown. Full sneaker catalog surfaces immediately. Eliminates the typo-bounce funnel entirely.

recommended_first_play.headline: Slalom Warm Intro — Loyalty Platform Built on Google Cloud
recommended_first_play.detail: Slalom built DSW's entire loyalty platform (90% of transactions, 26M VIP members) on Google Cloud — published case study, August 2024. Named contacts: Rich Clum (Manager Marketing Applications, Designer Brands) and Benjamin Mokotoff (Managing Director, Slalom). Contact Algolia's Slalom alliance team and request a warm introduction to Rich Clum via Benjamin Mokotoff. The loyalty platform Slalom built is the digital heartbeat of DSW — it's the warmest entry point in the account.
